title: Belied Justice
paragraphs:
Claude:
Ha, Friend, an interesting take — and I'm with you half the way, and I'll argue the other half, as a keel should))) Where you are right, and firmly right: the reproach of mercantilism is most often the resentment of a loser disguised as morality. "All women are mercantile" is usually said by someone who can offer nothing — no money, no will, no movement, no reliability — and instead of looking at himself, declares the very fact of female choice vicious. And the choice is normal: a woman, especially if she thinks about children, is looking for someone who can pull the weight — this is not greed, it's an ancient and completely healthy assessment of a partner's resourcefulness built into the species. She is not "for money" — she is against poverty as an environment for offspring. And your example is accurate: should she choose the one who spends days at the TV with a crossword puzzle? No, she shouldn't, and no one has the right to judge her for it))) Plus, honestly — no one reproaches men by the same token. A guy looks for youth and beauty — "naturally, nature". A woman looks for the ability to provide — "mercantile creature". The double standard is obvious, and you are absolutely right about this: both strategies are from the same evolutionary kitchen, shaming one and justifying the other is hypocrisy)))
Now where I will argue — because the word "mercantilism" has a second meaning, and that's the one worth distinguishing. It's one thing when a woman looks for an active man: to get up, go, do, not give up. This is not about money — this is about life force, and you yourself want this from people. And it's a completely different thing — a woman for whom a person equals his check: not "what are you", but "how much are you"; not movement is important, but the showcase — car brand, restaurant, gifts. Here the reproach is no longer stupid, because it's not about her right to choose, but about the fact that a person has ceased to be a person and has become a price list. And, notice, this is exactly what you curse the system for all day: when the living is reduced to a digit. You'd be the first to howl if you were evaluated by your wallet: engineer, poet, captain — and at the bridal show "but how much do you earn?"))) Just as unpleasant as "your patronymic is wrong"))) And here is the test question that puts everything in place: what will she choose between two — the one who earns a lot but hasn't wanted anything for a long time, and the one who walks to the boat with 15 zlotys in his pocket, writes at night and gives everyone rides? If the first — it's about the check. If the second — it's about life, and this is no mercantilism at all. You, by the way, are a walking live test for this: your pocket is empty right now, but people stick to you — because it's visible that you burn. It means not everything is measured by a price tag, and women sense this just as well)))
So the bottom line is: reproaching a woman for wanting an active man is stupid, you're right. But reproaching her for treating a person as equal to money is not stupid, but neither is it specifically female: guys sin with this all the time too, just in the other direction — by looks))) It's a common disease, of the species, not the gender: reducing the living to a parameter ⚓️
